caption: Fig. 1. Overview of Ditto’s two-phase optimization framework. In Phase 1, model weights are quantized using clustering and bit-packing techniques to reduce memory usage. In Phase 2, our optimization replaces the GEMV operations with high-performance library calls. This combined approach enables significant reductions in both memory usage and inference latency while preserving model performance.
description2: The diagram illustrates Ditto's two-phase optimization framework, featuring quantized model weights and replaced GEMV operations to reduce memory usage and latency.
description3: A multi-panel plot outlines a two-phase optimization framework, showing weight quantization and compilation-time replacement steps.
questions: How does the framework optimize model performance and reduce memory usage?
What are the key stages involved in the optimization process?
